name: Chowing's Tavern Brunswick Stew | (Hbwk07a)
recipeIngredient: 2 medium Potatoes - diced into half-inch pieces
4 cups Corn from cob or 2 (16 oz) can of corn
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken into pieces and simmer in 3 quarts of water until the meat is easily removable from the bones, approximately 2 hours.
2. Remove the cooked chicken and add raw vegetables to the pot. Simmer uncovered until the beans and potatoes are tender, stirring occasionally to avoid scorching.
3. Add the diced chicken back into the pot along with the seasonings. Mix well and allow the mixture to cool to room temperature before covering and refrigerating overnight.
4. Reheat over moderately-low heat until it reaches a slow simmer. Do not boil.
5. Serve with warm biscuits or bread.
Notes:
If canned vegetables are used, ensure that you drain them and add their juices to the chicken cooking liquid. Brunswick stew is best enjoyed after it has been aged for some time; in many households, it is not served immediately after preparation.
